title: Pauletia faberi
intro: Pauletia faberi, also known as Paullinia cupana, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Sapindaceae family. The plant's seeds, commonly referred to as guarana seeds, have been used for centuries due to their potential health benefits, including increased energy, improved cognitive function, and enhanced antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Paullinia cupana include alkaloids, such as theobromine and theophylline, as well as caffeine, which are responsible for its stimulant effects. Paullinia cupana is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and energy-boosting powders, often marketed as a natural energy supplement or to aid in weight loss.
